label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about what can be concluded from Isabelle Dinoire's remarks. The options are A to D. First, I need to look at the part where she talks about seeing herself in the mirror. The text says she was scared to look, but when she did, she said it was "marvelous" and "already beautiful." That suggests she was happy with how she looked after the transplant. So maybe B is a candidate here. Option A says she was too shocked to see herself. But the text mentions she was scared, but she did look and was happy. So A might not be correct because she did look and wasn't just shocked but actually felt positive. Option C says she thought her face couldn't be repaired. However, the text says doctors initially offered little hope with conventional surgery, but she had the transplant. Her own remarks don't indicate she thought it couldn't be fixed; she had the transplant and was satisfied. So C is probably not right. Option D claims she looks more beautiful than before. She said it was beautiful, but the text doesn't compare it to her
